main_event: Two persons in Constantine saw luminous object at high speed on 14 August 1952; same day red disk seen at Philippeville.
sighted_object: luminous obj flying at high speed; did not resemble a flying saucer; emitted a very bright light
witness_description: civilian, two persons in the city of Constantine
interesting_points: Luminous object flying at high speed toward Guelma at 2115 hours
Apparatus had no resemblance to a flying saucer and emitted a very bright light
Local weather bureau had not launched any weather balloons at the time
Same day at 1920 hours, many people at docks of Philippeville saw enormous red disk going north to west, leaving greenish trail
Case also includes clipping of saucer-like craft seen in Spain (Cuenca-Madrid road, 10 Aug 1952)
Case folder also contains North Pacific meteor observation from USS Norman Lykes, 12 Aug 1952
